lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: Hello again!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's with the duck?

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: Oh, it's part of a little test I'd like to do. A travelling salesman stopped off here a while ago and claimed you can use a duck to accurately track currents in the sea.

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: My plan is to track the currents around Catherby to see if I can speed up the docking process, but there's a bit of a snag...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: The local council have a by-law forbidding non-human workers from doing business in Catherby. I'd hoped the local councillor wouldn't notice, but the second the duck arrived, she came along making threats of a fine!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Anything I can do to help?

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: I don't think so, but thanks for the offer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Anything I can do to help?

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: Well, you're welcome to try talking to Councillor Catherine yourself. You'll find her in the Council Office north east of here. I warn you though, it will probably be an uphill struggle!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for the warning. I'll head over and see what I can do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry to hear that. Hopefully you manage to work something out.

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: Thank you, friend.

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: Hello again!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I find the Council Office?

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: It's just north east of here. Good luck trying to deal with Councillor Catherine though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there. Are you Councillor Catherine? I was hoping you could help me with something.

lines.speaker: Councillor Catherine
lines.text: Thank you for enquiring at the Catherby Council Office. Your views are important to us. What is it I can do for you today?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I had a question about one of the local by-laws. Apparently non-human workers aren't allowed to do business in Catherby?

lines.speaker: Councillor Catherine
lines.text: That is correct.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Doesn't that strike you as a bit... wrong?

lines.speaker: Councillor Catherine
lines.text: If you are referring to the rumours of Humans Against Monsters sympathies within the Catherby Council, then I can assure you that a full impartial investigation found those rumours to be totally false.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Impartial?

lines.speaker: Councillor Catherine
lines.text: Completely.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see... So you wouldn't even be willing to let Arhein's duck track a few currents? The results would be very useful, not just to Arhein. I imagine any sailor docking here would find them helpful.

lines.speaker: Councillor Catherine
lines.text: Are you a sailor yourself?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, yes, I suppose you could say that.

lines.speaker: Councillor Catherine
lines.text: All sailors doing business in Catherby must first be registered with the Catherby Council. Doing so requires completion of the appropriate form which is the fourth in the port series of the Catherby Registry form group.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait... What?

lines.speaker: Councillor Catherine
lines.text: Please ensure when filling in the form that all answers are clearly legible. Failure to do so may result in unnecessary processing delays.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But why do I need to...

lines.speaker: Councillor Catherine
lines.text: Thanks to recent budget changes, I'm pleased to confirm that the Catherby Council are now able to provide charcoal free of charge. If required, you can find some in the cabinet to my left.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right...

lines.speaker: Councillor Catherine
lines.text: Upon completion, please return the form to the Catherby Council Office at your earliest convenience.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Councillor Catherine
lines.text: Have you fully filled in that form?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I have it here.

lines.speaker: Councillor Catherine
lines.text: I am afraid there are still questions missing answers. Please make sure you fully fill the form in before you hand it in.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I have it here.

lines.speaker: Councillor Catherine
lines.text: Thank you very much. However, I must inform you that the Catherby Council is currently experiencing higher than usual volumes of enquiries, so there may be some delays in the processing of your form.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay... I don't actually really care about the form though.

lines.speaker: Councillor Catherine
lines.text: Then what is it I can help you with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That by-law about non-humans... I want to get it changed.

lines.speaker: Councillor Catherine
lines.text: Any change to the by-laws can only occur with approval from the Mayor of Catherby.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, we're getting somewhere. Where can I find the mayor?

lines.speaker: Councillor Catherine
lines.text: I'm afraid that due to the Catherby Data Protection Regulation, I am not at liberty to give out personal information on the mayor.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you serious?

lines.speaker: Councillor Catherine
lines.text: Very serious. Now, if that's all, thank you for visiting the Catherby Council Office today.

lines.speaker: Councillor Catherine
lines.text: We hope your experience was a positive one, but if you do have any feedback, I'd be more than happy to provide you with the relevant form.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah... I think I'll pass on that.

lines.speaker: Councillor Catherine
lines.text: Then I wish you a very good day.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Councillor Catherine
lines.text: Please ensure that you have done so before you hand it in. Please also ensure that all answers are clearly legible. Failure to do so may result in unnecessary processing delays.

lines.speaker: Councillor Catherine
lines.text: Thanks to recent budget changes, I'm pleased to confirm that the Catherby Council are now able to provide charcoal free of charge. If required, you can find some in the cabinet to my left.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay.

sections.title: Talking to Councillor Catherine after handing in form cr-4p

lines.speaker: Councillor Catherine
lines.text: Welcome to the Catherby Council Office. Your views are important to us. How can I help you today?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to speak to the mayor.

lines.speaker: Councillor Catherine
lines.text: I'm afraid that due to the Catherby Data Protection Regulation, I am not at liberty to give out personal information on the mayor. Now, if that's all, thank you for visiting the Catherby Council Office today.

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: Hello again!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to find the Mayor of Catherby.

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: The mayor? Oh no...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: I'm afraid to say that the mayor died a few days ago.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh... I'm sorry to hear that.

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: Don't worry, it happens all the time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It does? That feels like the kind of thing I should be worried about...

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: Why do you need the mayor anyway?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Apparently only the mayor can approve a change to the by-laws.

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: Really? I don't recall that ever stopping Catherine before, but if she insists, I guess we can do this song and dance once more.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Meaning?

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: We're going to need another mayor, but we need to make sure Catherine doesn't notice.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where on Gielinor will we get another mayor? And how in the name of Guthix's trousers will Catherine not notice?

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: Well, the Mayor of Catherby has been, for a long time now, a fish.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A fish?

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: Actually, to be precise, it has been 26 different fish, 25 of which were doing a fantastic job of pretending to be the first.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure this democracy thing is going to catch on if it keeps being implemented this badly.

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: Well, unfortunately no one in Catherby really wanted the job. In fact, there was only one candidate, and it was the one person we all definitely didn't want to be the mayor.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Councillor Catherine?

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: Councillor Catherine.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So the answer was a fish?

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: It seemed like a good idea at the time. Problem is, Catherine responded by introducing that by-law preventing non-humans from working here.

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: We were lucky that a different by-law she'd already introduced clearly stated that the mayor serves until death.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course. And as a result, you now need to make sure she doesn't know the mayor died.

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: Exactly!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll help you replace the fish.

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: A wise choice. Go and see Harry at the fishing shop. He should be able to help.

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: Hello again!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About the mayor...

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: You'll need to go and see Harry at the fishing shop. He should be able to help you sort out a replacement mayor.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know, out of context, that might be the strangest thing anyone has ever said.

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: Well, desperate times...

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.text: Welcome! If you're looking for fishing equipment, you're in the right place. I'll also give you a good price on any fish that you want to sell.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here about the mayor.

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.text: What about the mayor?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Arhein said you could help me get a replacement.

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.text: Oh, we've lost another one have we?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Afraid so.

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.text: Well, it's easy to fix. You just need a special mayoral fishbowl and a tiny net. Once you have them, grab a fish from one of the aquariums with the net, and that is our new mayor.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get the fishbowl and net?

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.text: I can sell you the whole kit for 50 coins. Just come back once you've got enough room.

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.text: I can sell you the whole kit for 50 coins.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't have enough money for that.

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.text: Politics is an expensive game!

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.text: I can sell you the whole kit for 50 coins.

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.text: There you go. Once you've caught the mayor, be sure to take him to Arhein to make sure he's happy. Just don't take too long. They don't seem to have a long life span...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I'll pass.

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.text: Politics is an expensive game!

sections.title: Talking to Harry after buying the mayoral election kit
Talking to Harry after buying and losing the kit

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.text: Welcome! If you're looking for fishing equipment, you're in the right place. I'll also give you a good price on any fish that you want to sell.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here about the mayor.

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.text: I think you dropped these. Good job I noticed and picked them up for you. Now, go grab a fish from one of the aquariums with the net, and that is our new mayor.

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.text: I think you dropped your net and fishbowl. Don't worry, I picked them up for you. Just make soom room for them and I'll give them back.

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.text: Welcome! If you're looking for fishing equipment, you're in the right place. I'll also give you a good price on any fish that you want to sell.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here about the mayor.

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.text: You've got your special mayoral fishbowl and tiny net. Just grab a fish from one of the aquariums with the net, and that is our new mayor.

sections.title: Talking to Harry after catching the Mayor

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.text: Welcome! If you're looking for fishing equipment, you're in the right place. I'll also give you a good price on any fish that you want to sell.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here about the mayor.

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.text: I see you've caught a new one. You'd best show him to Arhein. Just don't take too long. They don't seem to have a long life span...

sections.title: Talking to Arhein with the Mayor

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: Hello again!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About the mayor...

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The mayor has returned.

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: Long live the mayor.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what now?

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: Well, I think I'll start keeping a stock of mayors on standby from now on.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That seems like a good idea, but I meant about the by- law.

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: Ah, of course. Let me just give him his mayoral chain...

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: There! Catherine shouldn't be able to stop us getting the by-law changed now. You should go and speak with her.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll head over there right away.

sections.title: Talking to Arhein with the Mayor, again

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: Hello again!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About the mayor...

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do I need to do with him now?

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: You should speak to Catherine. Now that we have the mayor, she shouldn't be able to stop us getting that by-law changed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: In that case, I'll go and see her right away.

sections.title: Talking to Councillor Catherine without the Mayor

lines.speaker: Councillor Catherine
lines.text: Welcome to the Catherby Council Office. Your views are important to us. How can I help you today?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have the mayor!

lines.speaker: Councillor Catherine
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I did have the mayor, but I appear to have misplaced him.

lines.speaker: Councillor Catherine
lines.text: You've misplaced the mayor?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's right.

lines.speaker: Councillor Catherine
lines.text: I see... Well, if that's all, thank you for visiting the Catherby Council Office today.

sections.title: Talking to Councillor Catherine with the Mayor

lines.speaker: Councillor Catherine
lines.text: Welcome to the Catherby Council Office. Your views are important to us. How can I help you today?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have the mayor!

lines.speaker: Councillor Catherine
lines.text: Are you sure? It doesn't look like the mayor. Where is his mayoral chain?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know.

lines.speaker: Councillor Catherine
lines.text: Hmm... Well, if that's all, thank you for visiting the Catherby Council Office today.

lines.speaker: Councillor Catherine
lines.text: Oh, how wonderful.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, shall we get to work on getting that by-law changed?

lines.speaker: Councillor Catherine
lines.text: I'm afraid a recent audit has exposed irregularities in key paperwork filed with the Catherby Council Office. These irregularities must be addressed prior to any updates to the by-laws.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here we go...

lines.speaker: Councillor Catherine
lines.text: Unfortunately, as you yourself have recently filed a form with the Catherby Council Office, I must now ask you to join me in reviewing that form to ensure everything is correct.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What if I've forgotten what I put down?

lines.speaker: Councillor Catherine
lines.text: That seems unlikely. After all, you gave accurate information, did you not?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am shocked that you would suggest otherwise.

lines.speaker: Councillor Catherine
lines.text: Very good. In that case, let us begin.

lines.speaker: Councillor Catherine
lines.text: What is your main reason for making port at Catherby?

lines.speaker: Councillor Catherine
lines.text: How many hulls does your ship have?

lines.speaker: Councillor Catherine
lines.text: Is your ship insured against cargo spillage and theft?

lines.speaker: Councillor Catherine
lines.text: Does your first mate have first aid training?

lines.speaker: Councillor Catherine
lines.text: Does your second mate have second aid training?

lines.speaker: Councillor Catherine
lines.text: Have you experienced symptoms of plague in the last two weeks?

lines.speaker: Councillor Catherine
lines.text: How much experience do you have sailing?

lines.speaker: Councillor Catherine
lines.text: Where would you say your home port is?

lines.speaker: Councillor Catherine
lines.text: Hmm, that all seems to be right...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can we finally get on with this by-law change now?

lines.speaker: Councillor Catherine
lines.text: *sigh* Yes, I suppose everything is in order. I have a form here that needs signing by the mayor. Please make some room so that I am able to give it to you.

lines.speaker: Councillor Catherine
lines.text: *sigh* Yes, I suppose everything is in order. I just need this form signing by the mayor.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right... How does the mayor sign forms usually, given his lack of hands?

lines.speaker: Councillor Catherine
lines.text: Simply dip the form into his bowl and he will give it a nibble. We consider that to be his signature.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fair enough.

lines.speaker: Councillor Catherine
lines.text: I'm afraid that one of your answers didn't match the original form. We will have to go through the incorrect questions again.

sections.title: Talking to Councillor Catherine after giving all correct answers

lines.speaker: Councillor Catherine
lines.text: Welcome to the Catherby Council Office. Your views are important to us. How can I help you today?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can we finally get on with this by-law change now?

lines.speaker: Councillor Catherine
lines.text: *sigh* Yes, I suppose everything is in order. I have a form here that needs signing by the mayor. Please make some room so that I am able to give it to you.

lines.speaker: Councillor Catherine
lines.text: I suppose so. You just need to get that form signed by the mayor. Simply dip the form into his bowl and he will give it a nibble. We consider that to be his signature.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fair enough.

sections.title: Reclaiming the form after losing it

lines.speaker: Councillor Catherine
lines.text: Welcome to the Catherby Council Office. Your views are important to us. How can I help you today?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry, but I lost the form you gave me. Can I have another?

lines.speaker: Councillor Catherine
lines.text: Very well, but I would advise you take more care next time.

sections.title: Handing in the signed form to Councillor Catherine

lines.speaker: Councillor Catherine
lines.text: Welcome to the Catherby Council Office. Your views are important to us. How can I help you today?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have that signed form.

lines.speaker: Councillor Catherine
lines.text: The by-law has now been amended. Good day.

sections.title: Talking to Councillor Catherine after handing in the signed form

lines.speaker: Councillor Catherine
lines.text: Welcome to the Catherby Council Office. Your views are important to us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's good to hear...

lines.speaker: Councillor Catherine
lines.text: Unfortunately, all of our operatives are too busy to help you today. Please try again soon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Delightful...

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: Hello again!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The by-law has been changed!

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: Excellent! Thank you for sorting that out for me. However, I do have one more thing I'd appreciate some help with.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: I have some repairs I need to do on my ship. Would you mind taking the duck out to test the currents yourself?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course. How do I do it?

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: From what I've been told, you just need to release the duck, follow him while he tracks the currents, and then pick him up once he's done.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds good. Where should I release him?

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: I saw a good spot next to that island east of the docks. The one with the obelisk on it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, I'll sail over there right away.

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: Have fun!

sections.title: Talking to Arhein without the duck

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: Hello again!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About that duck...

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry, but I've lost him.

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: Yes, I noticed. Luckily for us, he came wandering back to me. Make some room to hold him and I'll give him back.

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: Yes, I noticed. Luckily for us, he came wandering back to me. Here you go.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: Hello again!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About that duck...

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do I need to do with him?

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: From what I've been told, you just need to release the duck, follow him while he tracks the currents, and then pick him up once he's done.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds good. Where should I release him?

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: I saw a good spot next to that island east of the docks. The one with the obelisk on it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, I'll sail over there right away.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There he goes! Now to follow him and see where these currents flow.

sections.title: Talking to Arhein after charting the current

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: Hello again!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've charted the currents!

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: Thank you so much, friend! I'm sorry for all the nonsense you had to deal with. Please, keep the duck as thanks. I have no more need of him, and he seems to really like you!

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: In fact, keep the mayor as well. It's not like I don't have plenty of spares.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah, on that note, I'm afraid I lost him.

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: Not to worry. As I say, I have plenty of spares.

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: I also have a little something else for you, but it appears you don't have space to take it. Please come back when you do.

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: I believe that puts us at mayor [X] now. Long live the mayor.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you!

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: Don't mention it. All the best!

sections.title: Talking to Arhein prior to 19 November 2025

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: Hello again!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's with the duck?

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: Oh, it's part of a little test I'd like to do. A travelling salesman stopped off here a while ago and claimed you can use a duck to accurately track currents in the sea.

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: My plan is to track the currents around Catherby to see if I can speed up the docking process, but there's a bit of a snag...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: The local council have a by-law forbidding non-human workers from doing business in Catherby. I'd hoped the local councillor wouldn't notice, but the second the duck arrived, she came along making threats of a fine!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Anything I can do to help?

lines.speaker: Arhein
lines.text: I don't think so, but thanks for the offer.
